observations.title: Testing infrastructure exploration for VeTube
observations.content:
**What**: Explored introducing testing infrastructure to VeTube — a Python/wxPython live stream GUI.

**Why**: The project has zero tests, no linter, no type checker, no formatter. CI only builds installers. Need a practical strategy.

**Where**: All modules — particularly `servicios/estadisticas_manager.py` (pure logic, best first target), `utils/funciones.py`, `utils/fajustes.py`, `globals/data_store.py` (import-time side effects), `setup.py` (hardware singletons at import), `.github/workflows/`, `pyproject.toml`.

**Learned**:
- **EstadisticasManager** is the ideal first test target: 99 lines, pure logic, zero wx/network imports, 100% coverage in under 1 hour.
- Import-time side effects in `setup.py` and `globals/data_store.py` are the #1 obstacle to testing. Solution: `conftest.py` sets `VETUBE_TEST_MODE=1` env var before any imports, `setup.py` guards hardware init behind it.
- No dependency injection anywhere — everything reaches into `from setup import player, reader`. Refactoring to DI is Phase 2+.
- Three approaches compared: Incremental (pytest + isolated tests first, no architecture changes), Full Isolation (protocol refactor + full harness), Hybrid (extract core + adapter boundaries). **Recommended: Incremental** — quick wins build momentum, no risk of breaking fragile wxPython/audio threading.
- ruff can be added trivially via `uv add --dev ruff` with zero production code changes.
- wxPython GUI testing on Windows CI is impractical without a display — tag wx-dependent tests with `pytest.mark.skipif` for now.

observations.title: sdd/testing-infrastructure/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Testing Infrastructure

## Intent
VeTube has zero testing infrastructure — no runner, no linter, no type checker. CI only builds binaries. Introduce the minimum viable testing toolchain so the project gains quality gates and a repeatable verification path.

## Scope
**In Scope**: pytest, ruff, ty (Astral type checker) as dev deps; VETUBE_TEST_MODE guard in setup.py; tests/ dir with conftest.py; unit tests for EstadisticasManager; test + lint + type-check CI job in betube64.yml

**Out of Scope**: Controller/service/UI tests; adapter refactors; integration tests; Linux CI; coverage thresholds

## Capabilities
**New**: testing-infrastructure — dev toolchain config, test layout, CI integration, import-time guard

**Modified**: None

## Approach
Incremental — add toolchain, guard side effects, test pure logic first, wire into CI. wx headless imports get @pytest.mark.skipif. ruff permissive initially. ty targets typed files with py.typed markers.

## Affected Areas
- pyproject.toml: dev deps, ruff/ty config
- setup.py: VETUBE_TEST_MODE guard
- tests/: new dir
- .github/workflows/betube64.yml: CI job

## Risks
- wx can't import headless → skipif
- ruff noise on legacy → permissive rules
- CI Python mismatch → install matching or skip

## Rollback
Revert pyproject.toml, delete tests/, revert setup.py, revert betube64.yml

## Dependencies
pytest, ruff, ty (via uv)

## Success Criteria
- pytest green on CI
- ruff check zero errors
- ty zero errors on typed files
- EstadisticasManager full coverage
- setup.py skips side effects under VETUBE_TEST_MODE

observations.title: sdd/testing-infrastructure/spec
observations.content:
# Testing Infrastructure Specification

## Purpose
Dev toolchain configuration, test layout, CI integration, and import-time guard for VeTube test isolation.

## Requirements

### Dev Dependencies
The project MUST declare pytest, ruff, and ty as dev dependencies in `pyproject.toml` under `[dependency-groups]` with compatible version pins.

**Scenario: Dev deps available**: GIVEN a clean checkout, WHEN `uv sync --dev` executes, THEN pytest, ruff, and ty are installed and available.

### Import-Time Side Effect Guard
`setup.py` MUST check `VETUBE_TEST_MODE` before initializing I/O-dependent hardware. When `VETUBE_TEST_MODE=1`, setup.py MUST skip all I/O initialization. conftest.py MUST set `VETUBE_TEST_MODE=1` via os.environ before any application imports.

**Scenario: Test mode suppresses I/O**: GIVEN VETUBE_TEST_MODE=1, WHEN setup.py is imported, THEN no audio/IO initialization occurs.

**Scenario: Normal mode initializes**: GIVEN VETUBE_TEST_MODE unset, WHEN setup.py is imported, THEN audio/IO initializes normally.

**Scenario: conftest blocks side effects**: GIVEN conftest.py loads first, WHEN any test imports app code, THEN VETUBE_TEST_MODE is already 1.

### Test Layout
All tests MUST reside in tests/ at project root. conftest.py MUST provide fixtures. EstadisticasManager tests MUST be in tests/test_estadisticas_manager.py.

### EstadisticasManager Tests
Every public method MUST be tested: agregar_mensaje, agregar_seguidor, agregar_unido, actualizar_megusta, agregar_compartida, to_dict, from_dict, resetear_estadisticas, initial state.

**Scenario: Round-trip serialization**: GIVEN instance with messages=2, followers=1, WHEN to_dict→from_dict on fresh instance, THEN fresh instance reports messages=2, followers=1.

**Scenario: Reset clears state**: GIVEN non-zero counters, WHEN resetear_estadisticas(), THEN all counters zero.

### Linting
ruff MUST pass with zero errors in CI. Config in [tool.ruff] in pyproject.toml. May use permissive rules initially.

### Type Checking
ty MUST pass with zero errors on typed files. Config in [tool.ty] in pyproject.toml. Initially check only annotated files.

### CI Integration
betube64.yml MUST include a test job on push/PR: uv sync --dev → pytest → ruff check → ty. wx tests use @pytest.mark.skipif for headless.

### Rollback
Revert pyproject.toml, delete tests/, revert setup.py, revert betube64.yml.

observations.title: sdd/testing-infrastructure/design
observations.content:
# Design: Testing Infrastructure

## Technical Approach
Add a dev toolchain (pytest, ruff, ty) via `[dependency-groups]` in `pyproject.toml`, guard `setup.py` I/O with a `VETUBE_TEST_MODE` env var, seed `tests/` with a focused `EstadisticasManager` suite, and wire test+lint+type-check into `betube64.yml`. Keep the production code surface area minimal — only add `to_dict`/`from_dict` to `EstadisticasManager` (a 12-line additive change required to satisfy the spec's round-trip scenario).

## Architecture Decisions
| Decision | Choice | Tradeoff |
|---|---|---|
| Test isolation mechanism | `VETUBE_TEST_MODE` env var in `setup.py` | One-line check, no refactor. Cost: relies on `setup.py` being the import surface — must stay in sync. |
| wx headless handling | `@pytest.mark.skipif(not os.environ.get("DISPLAY"), ...)` | stdlib only. Cost: Windows CI always has a display, so the skip is effectively a Linux/macOS guard. |
| Tool config location | All in `pyproject.toml` (`[tool.ruff]`, `[tool.ty]`) | One config source for uv project. Cost: ty's schema is still evolving. |
| CI scope | `betube64.yml` only (x64) | Lower iteration cost. x86 deferred to follow-up. |
| Serialization API | Add `to_dict`/`from_dict` to `EstadisticasManager` | Spec mandates them. Cost: small additive change to production code. |
| Python version in CI | Align all workflows to Python 3.13 | Matches `.python-version` and `pyproject.toml`. Fixes pre-existing drift. |

**Rejected alternatives**:
- Refactor `setup.py` to lazy-import hardware modules → too invasive for a tooling PR
- Use `pytest-wx` plugin → adds a dep, doesn't solve headless skip
- Move config to separate `ruff.toml`/`ty.toml` → fragments config hub
- Add test job to both workflows → doubles CI cost before validation
- Keep mixed versions → pre-existing drift unresolved

## Data Flow
N/A for tooling config. VETUBE_TEST_MODE path: test process start → conftest.py sets env var → test imports setup.py/app code → setup.py checks env var, skips hardware init.

## File Changes
| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pyproject.toml` | Modify | Dev deps + [tool.ruff] + [tool.ty] |
| `setup.py` | Modify | VETUBE_TEST_MODE guard |
| `servicios/estadisticas_manager.py` | Modify | Add to_dict/from_dict |
| `tests/__init__.py` | Create | Package marker |
| `tests/conftest.py` | Create | Set VETUBE_TEST_MODE=1, fixtures |
| `tests/test_estadisticas_manager.py` | Create | ~10 tests, 0 mocks |
| `.github/workflows/betube64.yml` | Modify | Add test job (pytest → ruff → ty) |
| `.github/workflows/betube32.yml` | Modify | Update Python to 3.13 |

## Testing Strategy
- Unit: EstadisticasManager (direct instantiation, no mocks)
- Lint: ruff check . (permissive: E,F,W, ignore E501)
- Type: ty check servicios/estadisticas_manager.py (narrow scope)
- Integration/E2E: Deferred

## Migration / Rollout
No data migration. Rollback = revert all touched files.

## Open Questions
- Spec typo: `agrega_mensaje` → `agregar_mensaje`, `reset` → `resetear_estadisticas`
- `to_dict`/`from_dict` addition: required by spec round-trip scenario. If zero prod-code PR is preferred, drop the round-trip test and update spec.
- Python version drift: resolved — all workflows to 3.13.

**What**: Added comprehensive ruff ignore list (20+ rules) to pyproject.toml to handle legacy codebase noise

observations.title: Exploration: Send chat messages in live streams
observations.content:
**What**: Explored adding send-message capability to VeTube across all platforms
**Why**: User requested ability to send chat messages during live stream viewing
**Where**: servicios/tiktok.py, servicios/kick.py, servicios/youtube.py, servicios/twich.py, servicios/sala.py, controller/chat_controller.py, ui/chat_ui.py, ui/chat_dialog.py, keymaps/keys.txt
**Learned**: TikTokLive client has NO send API (read-only WebSocket). Only Kick's kick.py library has `Chatroom.send(content)` natively. YouTube/Twitch (chat-downloader/pytchat) are read-only. Blocking questions remain about authentication and platform priority.

**Why**: User requested ability to send messages to live streams

observations.content:
**What**: Investigated the Favorites/Followed Channels feature and wxPython 4.2.5 compatibility in VeTube

**Why**: User requested a codebase exploration for both topics

**Where**: globals/data_store.py, controller/main_controller.py, controller/menus/chat_menu_controller.py, ui/main_window.py, servicios/tiktok.py, run_main_window.py, data.json, favoritos.json

observations.title: sdd/live-status-monitor/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Live Status Monitor

## Intent

Users manually check if their favorite streamers are live across 5 platforms. This is tedious and unreliable. Add continuous live-status monitoring with visual indicators, notifications, and one-click join — this changes how users interact with the app daily.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Manual favorites management (URL paste + Add button in main window)
- `is_live()` per platform: TikTok (existing), YouTube, Twitch, Kick, La Sala (implement)
- Background polling thread with configurable interval (default 10 min, range 1-60 min)
- Visual indicator: red circle + "EN VIVO" label in favorites list
- Auto-sort: live channels first
- Windows toast notification (`wx.adv.NotificationMessage`) on go-live
- Sound alert on go-live, clickable notification opens chat
- Config: polling interval, notification/sound toggles in `data.json`

### Out of Scope
- Persistent chat history, keyword alerts, separate audio devices, message sending

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
- `live-monitor`: live-status detection, background polling, visual indicators, notifications, favorites management

### Modified Capabilities
None

## Approach

Add a `LiveMonitor` service with a polling timer. Each platform implements `is_live(url)`. Favorites UI gets enhanced with manual add, live indicators, and auto-sort via `wx.CallAfter`. Notifications use existing `wx.adv.NotificationMessage` infrastructure.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `globals/data_store.py` | Modified | New config keys (polling_interval, notify_on_live, sound_on_live) |
| `globals/favoritos.json` | Modified | Schema enhancement |
| `ui/main_window.py` | Modified | Favorites tab: URL input + Add button; live indicators |
| `controller/main_controller.py` | Modified | Wire polling, favorites CRUD |
| `controller/chat_controller.py` | Modified | Notification click → join chat |
| `servicios/tiktok.py` | Modified | Use existing `is_live()` |
| `servicios/youtube.py` | Modified | Implement `is_live()` |
| `servicios/twitch.py` | Modified | Implement `is_live()` |
| `servicios/kick.py` | Modified | Implement `is_live()` |
| `servicios/sala.py` | Modified | Implement `is_live()` |
| `services/live_monitor.py` | New | Background polling, notification dispatch |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Platform API rate limits | Med | Min interval 1 min, cache results |
| YouTube/Twitch need API keys | Med | Fallback to yt-dlp metadata parsing |
| Notification click varies by Windows version | Low | Test on Win10/Win11 |
| Polling thread safety with wx | Low | Use `wx.CallAfter` for UI updates |

## Rollback Plan

Revert all platform `is_live()` additions, remove `LiveMonitor` service, revert config schema, revert favorites UI changes.

## Dependencies

None

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Favorites can be added manually (URL paste) and during chat
- [ ] Live status refreshes at configured interval
- [ ] Live channels show red circle + "EN VIVO"
- [ ] Live channels sorted first in list
- [ ] Toast notification appears when channel goes live
- [ ] Clicking notification opens the chat
- [ ] Sound plays when channel goes live

observations.title: SDD Exploration - ChatService Architecture Analysis
observations.content:
**What**: Comprehensive exploration of service instantiation, message routing duplication, controller coupling, threading patterns, and refactor impact across 5 chat services (YouTube, Twitch, TikTok, Kick, Sala)
**Why**: To assess the architecture and plan a ChatService protocol/refactor
**Where**: controller/main_controller.py, controller/chat_controller.py, servicios/{tiktok,kick,youtube,twich,sala}.py, servicios/YouTubeRealDataTime.py, ui/chat_dialog.py, controller/menus/{chat_menu_controller,chat_item_controller}.py
**Learned**: All 5 services share identical constructor signature but have 3 distinct threading models (asyncio+Thread, plain Thread, Timer). Message routing is duplicated ~5x with the same config/sound/reader pattern. Controller accesses servicio.url and servicio.detener() as informal protocol, plus Twitch-specific servicio.chat.status. A protocol refactor would touch 11 files across 4 directories.

observations.title: sdd/service-protocol-refactor/spec
observations.content:
# Service Contract Specification

## Purpose

Formal interface contract and shared message routing for the five VeTube chat platform services (TikTok, Kick, YouTube, Twitch, Sala). Eliminates duplicated routing logic and establishes thread-safety guarantees.

## Requirements

### Requirement: ChatService Protocol

The system MUST define a `ChatService` protocol in `servicios/chat_service_protocol.py`. Every platform service (ServicioTiktok, ServicioKick, ServicioYouTube, ServicioTwich, ServicioSala) MUST conform to this protocol.

The protocol MUST declare: `url: str`, `iniciar_chat()`, `detener()`, `estadisticas_manager`, `chat_controller`, `media_controller`. It MAY declare optional members: `is_live()`, `enviar_mensaje()`.

#### Scenario: Protocol defined and type-checked
- GIVEN `servicios/chat_service_protocol.py` exists with the protocol definition
- WHEN `ty check` runs on all service files
- THEN no type errors related to ChatService conformance are reported

#### Scenario: All five services conform
- GIVEN each service file imports ChatService
- WHEN each service class is checked against the protocol
- THEN ServicioTiktok, ServicioKick, ServicioYouTube, ServicioTwich, and ServicioSala all satisfy the protocol

### Requirement: MessageRouter

The system SHOULD extract message routing into a shared `MessageRouter` class in `servicios/message_router.py`.

The router MUST check `data_store.config` toggles (`eventos`, `categorias`, `sonidos`, `reader`, `unread`), play sounds via `player.play`, read messages via `reader.leer_mensaje`, and dispatch to the correct ListBox method (`agregar_mensaje_general`, `agregar_mensaje_miembro`, `agregar_mensaje_moderador`, `agregar_mensaje_donacion`, `agregar_mensaje_verificado`, `agregar_mensaje_evento`).

The router MUST accept a `RoutableMessage` dataclass with: `text`, `author`, `category`, `event_type`, `platform`, `is_past`. Each service SHOULD build a `RoutableMessage` and delegate routing instead of inline config/sound/reader/ListBox blocks.

#### Scenario: Router dispatches to correct ListBox
- GIVEN a RoutableMessage with category="moderator"
- WHEN the router processes it
- THEN the message appears in `list_box_moderadores` when `eventos[4]` and `categorias[4]` are enabled

#### Scenario: Router respects config toggles
- GIVEN `eventos[0]` is disabled and a general message arrives
- WHEN the router processes it
- THEN no sound plays and no TTS reads for that message

#### Scenario: Identical routing across platforms
- GIVEN an equivalent chat message arrives on TikTok and on Kick
- WHEN each service builds a RoutableMessage and delegates to the router
- THEN resulting sound/reader/ListBox dispatch matches pre-refactor behavior

### Requirement: Threading Safety

YouTube (`recibir`) and Twitch chat loops run in background threads. Both services MUST wrap ALL UI calls in `wx.CallAfter`. Direct UI calls from threads (`player.play`, `reader.leer_mensaje`, `chat_controller` methods, wx.Dialog creation) MUST NOT occur without `wx.CallAfter`. The MessageRouter MUST be thread-safe or only invoked via `wx.CallAfter`.

#### Scenario: YouTube message from thread
- GIVEN ServicioYouTube running `recibir()` in a background thread
- WHEN a chat message arrives
- THEN all UI updates execute via `wx.CallAfter`

#### Scenario: Twitch message from thread
- GIVEN ServicioTwich chat loop running in a background thread
- WHEN any chat message triggers routing
- THEN no wxPython object is mutated without `wx.CallAfter`

### Requirement: Chat Menu Status Fix

`controller/menus/chat_menu_controller.py` MUST NOT assume `servicio.chat.status` exists. Each service SHOULD expose a `status` attribute that `chat_menu_controller` can safely query without AttributeError.

#### Scenario: No crash without chat_downloader
- GIVEN ServicioTiktok or ServicioSala is the active service
- WHEN `chat_menu_controller.addFavoritos` checks service status
- THEN no AttributeError occurs — the check degrades safely

#### Scenario: Twitch past status still detected
- GIVEN ServicioTwich with `chat.status == 'past'`
- WHEN `chat_menu_controller.addFavoritos` checks status
- THEN the title is correctly extracted via `funciones.extractUser(url)`

observations.title: sdd/service-protocol-refactor/design
observations.content:
# Design: Chat Service Protocol & Message Router Refactor

## Technical Approach

Two-phase incremental refactor. **Phase 1** adds a `typing.Protocol` type and annotates the 5 service classes — zero runtime change, pure type safety. **Phase 2** extracts the duplicated message-routing pipeline (config → sound → reader → ListBox) into a shared `MessageRouter`, refactoring one service at a time, and wrapping the YouTube/Twitch/Sala/`YouTubeRealDataTime` UI calls in `wx.CallAfter` as each service is touched.

## Architecture Decisions

### Decision: Protocol vs ABC
**Choice**: `typing.Protocol` (structural subtyping). **Alternative**: `abc.ABC` (nominal, forces explicit inheritance). **Rationale**: TikTok, Kick, YT, Twitch, Sala already share shape organically; Protocol accepts them as-is without altering their (already-divergent) class hierarchies or threading models. Same `ty` checking, zero coupling.

### Decision: MessageRouter as injected class
**Choice**: `MessageRouter(chat_controller, player, reader, config_provider)` with dependencies at init. **Alternative**: Module-level functions reading `data_store` directly. **Rationale**: Per-service testability — unit tests can pass a mock chat_controller/player/reader. Also unblocks future config-injection refactor.

### Decision: Single `RoutableMessage` dataclass
**Choice**: One dataclass with `text`, `author`, `category`, `event_type`, `platform`, `is_past`, `event_index`. **Alternative**: Union of platform-specific message types. **Rationale**: The routing logic is identical across platforms — only the *classification* differs. A single shape keeps the router signature trivial and avoids type-explosion.

### Decision: Router wraps UI calls in `wx.CallAfter` internally
**Choice**: Router invokes `wx.CallAfter(player.play, ...)`, `wx.CallAfter(reader.leer_mensaje, ...)`, `wx.CallAfter(chat_controller.agregar_mensaje_*, ...)`. **Alternative**: Require callers to wrap before invoking. **Rationale**: YouTube/Twitch/Sala/YouTubeRealDataTime all run routing inside a background thread. Forcing every service to remember the wrap is exactly the bug we are fixing. Centralizing thread-safety in the router eliminates the entire class of bug.

### Decision: Phase order (Protocol → Router)
**Choice**: Two separate PRs. **Alternative**: Single PR. **Rationale**: Phase 1 is a no-op at runtime (revertable trivially), gives reviewers a checkpoint, and lets `ty` validate the protocol surface before refactoring logic on top of it.

## Data Flow (Phase 2)

```
Platform lib → service callback
   ├─ build RoutableMessage (text, author, category, event_type, is_past)
   └─ router.route(msg)
        ├─ category → (eventos_idx, categorias_idx, listasonidos_idx, unread_idx)
        ├─ eventos[i] AND categorias[i] AND ui-has-listbox  → else: drop
        ├─ sonidos AND listasonidos[i] AND not is_past       → wx.CallAfter(player.play)
        ├─ reader AND unread[i]                             → wx.CallAfter(reader.leer_mensaje)
        └─ wx.CallAfter(chat_controller.agregar_mensaje_<category>)
```

## File Changes

| File | Action | Phase | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `servicios/chat_service_protocol.py` | Create | 1 | ~25-line `ChatService(Protocol)` |
| `servicios/tiktok.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Annotate `: ChatService`; build `RoutableMessage` in each `on_*`; replace inline blocks with `router.route(msg)` |
| `servicios/kick.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Same |
| `servicios/youtube.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Same + wrap remaining `player.play` in `wx.CallAfter` |
| `servicios/twich.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Same + wrap all `player.play`/`reader.leer_mensaje`/ListBox calls in `wx.CallAfter` |
| `servicios/sala.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Same + wrap calls (Timer runs off-main-thread) |
| `servicios/YouTubeRealDataTime.py` | Modify | 1+2 | Same + wrap `player.play` calls |
| `servicios/message_router.py` | Create | 2 | `RoutableMessage` dataclass + `MessageRouter` class + `CATEGORY_INDICES` map |
| `controller/main_controller.py` | Modify | 1 | Type `servicio: ChatService` after the if/elif chain |
| `controller/menus/chat_menu_controller.py` | Modify | 1 | Replace `servicio.chat.status` with `getattr(servicio, 'status', None)` |

## Interfaces / Contracts

```python
# Phase 1
class ChatService(Protocol):
    url: str
    chat_controller: 'ChatController'
    estadisticas_manager: 'EstadisticasManager'
    media_controller: Optional['MediaController']
    def iniciar_chat(self) -> None: ...
    def detener(self) -> None: ...

# Phase 2
@dataclass
class RoutableMessage:
    text: str
    author: str
    category: str        # 'general'|'miembro'|'moderador'|'verificado'|'donacion'|'evento'
    event_type: Optional[str] = None   # 'chest'|'follow'|'join'|'like'|'share'|'subscribe'|...
    platform: str = ''
    is_past: bool = False
    event_index: int = 0  # fallback for events

class MessageRouter:
    def __init__(self, chat_controller, player, reader, config_provider): ...
    def route(self, msg: RoutableMessage) -> None: ...
```

Router uses `CATEGORY_INDICES = {'general':0,'miembro':1,'donacion':3,'moderador':4,'verificado':5}` to map category → `data_store.config['eventos'|'categorias'|'listasonidos'|'unread']` indices. For `'evento'` category, uses `msg.event_index` instead. For `moderador`, plays `rutasonidos[4]` (or `[7]` for propietario — router gets a `sound_index` override field). For 'past' streams, skip sound.

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What | How |
|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Type conformance | `ty check` over all service files |
| Phase 2 | Routing output parity | Per-service side-by-side: capture pre-refactor `RoutableMessage` traces, run new router, assert identical dispatch |
| Phase 2 | Thread safety | Manual live test on YT past/live + Twitch sub/cheer; verify no `wx._core.PyAssertionError` |
| Regression | Existing suite | All 24 `EstadisticasManager` tests + manual smoke per platform |

## Migration / Rollout

No data migration. Two-PR phased rollout: PR-1 lands Protocol + annotations + chat_menu_controller fix (zero behavior change). PR-2 lands `MessageRouter` + service refactors + `wx.CallAfter` fixes (one service per commit for bisect-ability).

## Open Questions

None. Exploration mapped all 5 services, 3 listbox-dispatch methods, 4 `agregar_mensaje_evento` call sites, and the `chat.status` assumption surface.

observations.title: Bumped numpy to >=2.4.0 for Python 3.13 compatibility
observations.content:
**What**: Changed numpy from pinned 1.26.4 to `>=2.4.0,<3` for Python 3.13/3.14 compatibility
**Why**: numpy 1.26.4 has no wheels for Python 3.13 → CI fails. numpy 2.4.6 has cp313 and cp314 wheels for all platforms.
**Where**: pyproject.toml, uv.lock
**Learned**: numpy 1.x ended at Python 3.12. numpy 2.4.0+ supports >=3.11. onnxruntime works with numpy 2.x without issues. numpy is only used in TTS/Piper/__init__.py and cannot be easily removed because onnxruntime natively uses numpy arrays for model I/O.

observations.title: Fixed DeprecationWarning for WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy in Python 3.14+
observations.content:
**What**: Guarded asyncio.WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy call with version check to only run on Python < 3.14
**Why**: The policy is deprecated in 3.14, causes DeprecationWarning, and will be removed in 3.16. The app already works without it on 3.14.5 (user confirmed).
**Where**: run_main_window.py:8-9
**Learned**: `asyncio.WindowsSelectorEventLoopPolicy` was historically needed for wxPython compatibility on Windows (ProactorEventLoop had issues), but on Python 3.14+ the default Windows event loop policy works correctly with wxPython, so the override is no longer needed.

observations.title: sdd/service-polymorphic-refactor/explore
observations.content:
## Exploration: Service Architecture — Polymorphic Validation & Modern Python Refactor

### Current State

The VeTube service layer consists of 6 platform services + supporting infrastructure:

**Services (each in a single monolithic file):**
- `servicios/tiktok.py` (259 lines) — `ServicioTiktok`: async TikTokLive client, event handlers, media player init, currency conversion, translator, stats
- `servicios/kick.py` (218 lines) — `ServicioKick`: Kick client, bypass subprocess management, badge-based message routing, media player
- `servicios/youtube.py` (186 lines) — `ServicioYouTube`: ChatDownloader sync loop, live/past detection, badge parsing, currency conversion, service switching to YouTubeRealTime
- `servicios/twich.py` (215 lines) — `ServicioTwich`: ChatDownloader sync loop, bits/cheer parsing, subscription events, badge routing
- `servicios/sala.py` (76 lines) — `ServicioSala`: PlayroomHelper timer polling, basic message routing
- `servicios/YouTubeRealDataTime.py` (137 lines) — `YouTubeRealTimeService`: pytchat async polling, currency conversion, badge routing, media player

**Supporting files:**
- `servicios/chat_service_protocol.py` — `@runtime_checkable` Protocol defining `url`, `chat_controller`, `estadisticas_manager`, `media_controller`, `iniciar_chat()`, `detener()`
- `servicios/message_router.py` — `MessageRouter` class + `RoutableMessage` dataclass: centralized config→sound→TTS→ListBox pipeline
- `servicios/estadisticas_manager.py` — `EstadisticasManager`: user tracking, counter management, JSON serialization
- No `__init__.py` exists in `servicios/`

**Common constructor signature across all services:**
```python
def __init__(self, main_controller, url, frame, plataforma, chat_controller):
```

### Validation Duplication

1. **Currency conversion** — identical pattern in 4/6 services (TikTok, YouTube, Twitch, YouTubeRealTime) with minor variations.
2. **Message translation** — identical `if data_store.dst: self.translator.translate(...)` in 5/6 services.
3. **Badge-based role routing** — duplicated logic with different data structures across all platforms.
4. **RoutableMessage construction** — same category routing repeated for every event handler.

### SRP Violations

Services do: network I/O, event parsing, currency conversion, translation, media controller setup, statistics tracking, and lifecycle management — all in one class. YouTube service even instantiates another service (YouTubeRealTime) and swaps it on the controller.

### Recommended Approach

**Moderate refactor (Approach 2):** Split each service into module packages, expand protocol, extract shared utilities, introduce BaseService ABC, use pattern matching. Gives 80% benefit for 50% effort compared to full polymorphic architecture.

### Key Findings
- 6 services share identical constructor signature — polymorphic instantiation already possible
- `ChatService` protocol exists but is minimal (only 2 methods + 4 attributes)
- No services have tests — highest risk area
- Spanish method names are deeply embedded across controllers
- `wx.CallAfter` threading pattern is pervasive — any refactor must preserve thread safety
- Pattern matching (`match/case`) can cleanly replace badge-based if-elif chains

### Affected Areas
All 6 service files, protocol, message_router, both controllers, tests

### Risks
Breaking backward compat, thread safety regression, currency conversion display errors, scope creep from method renaming

observations.title: Service Polymorphic Refactor — Proposal
observations.content:
**What**: Wrote proposal.md for service polymorphic refactor — split 6 monolithic platform services into packages with BaseChatService ABC, _ui_call() threading, English naming, test-first approach.
**Why**: Eliminate duplicated currency/translation/badge parsing, SRP violations, inconsistent method naming, and ad-hoc wx.CallAfter threading across all 6 platform services.
**Where**: openspec/changes/service-polymorphic-refactor/proposal.md, sdd/service-polymorphic-refactor/proposal (Engram)
**Learned**: (1) Sala is the ideal pilot — 76 lines, simplest service, validates package structure before complex services. (2) Controllers only call iniciar_chat() in one place (main_controller.py:298), so rename impact is small. (3) YouTubeRealTime has a unique iniciar_chat_reutilizando_ui() that needs special handling. (4) Two existing specs (service-contract, testing-infrastructure) provide baseline — service-contract needs a delta, service-validation and service-lifecycle are new capabilities.

observations.title: sdd/service-polymorphic-refactor/spec
observations.content:
# service-polymorphic-refactor — Specs (Combined)

## Domain: service-lifecycle (NEW)

### Purpose
Define the `BaseChatService` ABC contract: lifecycle states, threading model, and the centralized `_ui_call()` wrapper.

### Requirements

**R-LC-001: BaseChatService ABC**
The system MUST provide `BaseChatService(ABC)` in `servicios/base.py`. All platform services MUST inherit from it.

| ID | Requirement | Priority |
|----|-------------|----------|
| R-LC-001 | `BaseChatService(ABC)` exists in `servicios/base.py` | Must-have |
| R-LC-002 | All 6 platform services inherit from `BaseChatService` | Must-have |

Scenario: Sala service inherits BaseChatService
- GIVEN `BaseChatService` is defined
- WHEN `ServicioSala(BaseChatService)` is checked
- THEN `isinstance(servicio_sala, BaseChatService)` is True

**R-LC-002: Lifecycle Methods**
The ABC MUST define `start()`, `stop()`, and `receive()` as abstract methods. Lifecycle states: created → started → running → stopped.

| ID | Method | Contract | Priority |
|----|--------|----------|----------|
| R-LC-002a | `start()` | Sets `_running=True`, spawns worker thread, returns `None` | Must-have |
| R-LC-002b | `stop()` | Sets `_running=False`, releases media, stops loop/thread, idempotent | Must-have |
| R-LC-002c | `receive()` | Message reception loop; MUST NOT be called from main thread | Must-have |

Scenario: Double stop is safe — no exception and no side effects.
Scenario: start sets `_running=True` before thread execution begins.

**R-LC-003: `_ui_call()` Centralized Wrapper**
Base class MUST provide `_ui_call(callable, *args)` wrapping every UI update via `wx.CallAfter`. ALL UI mutations from threads MUST go through this method. Direct `wx.CallAfter` calls in services MUST NOT exist after refactor.

| ID | Check | Priority |
|----|-------|----------|
| R-LC-003a | `_ui_call()` exists as concrete method on `BaseChatService` | Must-have |
| R-LC-003b | Zero direct `wx.CallAfter` calls in any service after refactor | Must-have |
| R-LC-003c | Exception in callable is caught and logged, never propagated | Should-have |

Scenario: Chat message dispatched via `_ui_call(self.router.route, msg)` instead of `wx.CallAfter(...)`.
Scenario: Error in UI callable does not crash worker thread.

**R-LC-004: Thread Safety Guarantees**
`_ui_call` MUST schedule the callable on the wx main thread. `receive()` runs on a worker thread. Services MUST NOT mutate UI objects directly from `receive()`.
Scenario: `wx.IsMainThread()` returns False inside `receive()`.

**R-LC-005: Error Handling Contract**
`start()` MUST catch exceptions and call `notify_error`. `receive()` MUST catch per-message exceptions, report via `_ui_call(notify_error,...)`, and continue. `stop()` MUST NOT raise exceptions.
Scenario: Receive loop survives per-message KeyError during badge parsing.

---

## Domain: service-validation (NEW)

### Purpose
Shared pure-function modules: currency conversion, message translation, badge/role detection.

### Requirements

**R-VL-001: CurrencyConverter**
`servicios/shared/currency.py` MUST expose `convert(amount: float, source: str, target_currency: str) -> tuple[float | None, str | None]`.

| ID | Behavior | Priority |
|----|----------|----------|
| R-VL-001a | Converts via google_currency | Must-have |
| R-VL-001b | USD target: returns `(amount/100, "USD")` no API call | Must-have |
| R-VL-001c | "Por defecto" target: returns `(None, None)` — skip | Must-have |
| R-VL-001d | API error: returns `(None, error_msg)`, never crashes | Must-have |

Scenario: USD target skips API, returns `(5.0, "USD")`.
Scenario: API failure returns `(None, "ConnectionError")`.

**R-VL-002: MessageTranslator**
`servicios/shared/translator.py` MUST expose `translate(text: str, target_lang: str) -> str`. Returns translated text or original on failure.

| ID | Behavior | Priority |
|----|----------|----------|
| R-VL-002a | Wraps translator.translatorWrapper().translate() | Must-have |
| R-VL-002b | Error: returns original text + logs warning | Must-have |
| R-VL-002c | Empty/None input: returns "" | Should-have |

Scenario: Translation failure returns original text unchanged.

**R-VL-003: BadgeParser**
`servicios/shared/badge_parser.py` MUST expose `parse_role(author_data: dict, platform: str) -> str`. Returns: `"owner"`, `"moderator"`, `"subscriber"`, `"verified"`, `"general"`. Priority: owner > moderator > subscriber > verified > general.

| Platform | Badge Source | Priority |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| YouTube | `author["badges"]` list-of-dicts | Must-have |
| Twitch | badges + `is_moderator`/`is_subscriber` | Must-have |
| Kick | badges dict/object with `.type` | Must-have |
| YouTubeRT | `isChatOwner`/`isChatModerator`/`isChatSponsor`/`isVerified` | Must-have |
| TikTok/Sala | no badges → always `"general"` | Should-have |

Scenario: YouTube Owner badge → "owner". Kick subscriber → "subscriber". Unknown platform → "general".

**R-VL-004: Module Independence**
All three modules MUST be pure functions: zero wxPython, zero `data_store` globals, importable without wx.App.
Scenario: `from servicios.shared.currency import convert` succeeds without wx.App.

---

## Domain: service-testing (NEW)

### Purpose
Test structure, mandatory scenarios, and test-first workflow for service packages.

### Requirements

**R-TS-001: Test File Structure**
Each service gets: `tests/{platform}/test_client.py`, `test_handlers.py`, `test_validation.py`. Shared: `tests/shared/test_currency.py`, `test_translator.py`, `test_badge_parser.py`.

| Test File | Coverage | Priority |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| `test_currency.py` | CurrencyConverter all branches | Must-have |
| `test_translator.py` | translate() success+error | Must-have |
| `test_badge_parser.py` | parse_role() all 6 platforms | Must-have |
| `{platform}/test_client.py` | start/stop/receive lifecycle | Must-have |
| `{platform}/test_handlers.py` | match/case exhaustiveness | Must-have |
| `test_message_router.py` | route() config toggles, dispatch | Must-have |
| `test_protocol.py` | ChatService conformance | Should-have |

**R-TS-002: Mandatory Test Scenarios**
Per-service tests MUST cover: start spawns thread, stop idempotent + media release, one happy path per category (general/member/moderator/donation/verified/event), malformed message survival, _ui_call exclusivity.

**R-TS-003: Shared Utility Tests**
CurrencyConverter: USD pass-through, custom currency, API fallback, "Por defecto" skip. MessageTranslator: success, failure, empty string. BadgeParser: all 6 platforms + unknown fallback.

**R-TS-004: MessageRouter Tests**
Config toggle respect, sound suppression for is_past, correct listbox dispatch, TTS trigger.

**R-TS-005: Protocol Conformance Tests**
`isinstance(service, ChatService)` must be True for all 6 services.

**R-TS-006: Test-First Mandate**
Tests MUST be written BEFORE implementation. Order: shared → Sala pilot → remaining 5.

---

## Domain: service-contract (DELTA)

### MODIFIED: ChatService Protocol
Protocol now declares English methods: `start()`, `stop()`, `receive()`. BaseChatService ABC with `_ui_call()`. All services use `_ui_call` instead of direct `wx.CallAfter`.
(Previously: `iniciar_chat()`, `detener()` without ABC or centralized wrapper)

### MODIFIED: Threading Safety
All UI calls must go through `self._ui_call()`. Direct `wx.CallAfter` forbidden in services.
(Previously: direct `wx.CallAfter` calls in each service)

### ADDED: Backward Compatibility Re-exports
`servicios/__init__.py` provides deprecated aliases with `DeprecationWarning`. Old names map to new: `iniciar_chat`→`start`, `detener`→`stop`, `recibir`→`receive`, `agregar_mensaje_X`→`add_message_X`, `notificar_error`→`notify_error`, `agregar_seguidor`→`add_follower`.

### ADDED: Lifecycle State Contract
`_running: bool` initialized False. start() sets True before thread. stop() sets False, idempotent. receive() checks _running each iteration.

### RENAMED: Spanish methods → English
`iniciar_chat()→start()`, `detener()→stop()`, `recibir()→receive()`, `agregar_mensaje_X()→add_message_X()`, `notificar_error()→notify_error()`, `agregar_seguidor()→add_follower()`.
Internal: `_detener→_running` (inverted), `_hilo→_thread`.
Migration: Update callers in main_controller, chat_controller, chat_menu_controller.

observations.title: Service Polymorphic Refactor — Design
observations.content:
# sdd/service-polymorphic-refactor/design

## Technical Approach

Moderate-split refactor: 6 monolithic platform services (`tiktok.py`, `kick.py`, `youtube.py`, `twich.py`, `sala.py`, `YouTubeRealDataTime.py`) → per-platform packages with `__init__.py` (re-export) + `client.py` (network + lifecycle) + `handlers.py` (event → `RoutableMessage`). Shared validation (currency, translation, badge parsing) → pure-function modules under `servicios/shared/`. `BaseChatService(ABC)` owns lifecycle + shared state + centralized `_ui_call()`. Method names renamed to English. if-elif chains in router and YouTube badge dispatcher become `match/case`. Backward compat via re-exports in `servicios/__init__.py`. No observable behavior change.

## Architecture Decisions (rationale + alternatives)

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Per-service package layout | `__init__/client/handlers` per platform | 80% SRP/testability benefit, minimum diff. `__init__.py` re-export = backward-compat at import level |
| `BaseChatService(ABC)` not Protocol-only | ABC with concrete `_ui_call`, abstract lifecycle | `_ui_call` is concrete implementation — Protocols can't carry it. ABC = single audit point for "no wx.CallAfter outside base" |
| English method names, hard rename + re-exports | All public rename, `servicios/__init__.py` deprecation bridge | 2 callers (main_controller, chat_controller) need import swap; old names remain importable with DeprecationWarning. `_detener`→`_running` (inverted, standard Python convention) |
| Shared modules are pure functions | currency/translator/badge_parser accept everything as args; `data_store.dst` read once in service | Pure = importable without wx.App = testable = R-VL-004 |
| `match/case` only where it improves clarity | Router dispatch + YouTube badge routing only | Python 3.10+ exhaustiveness only matters in routing, not every branch |
| MessageRouter stays put, no redesign | Router preserved; only internal dispatch → `match/case` | Router is already correct; spec keeps it. Refactoring = scope creep |
| Backward-compat via `servicios/__init__.py` | `__getattr__` re-exports each service class with old name | Tests + main_controller keep working. `DeprecationWarning` = signal without breakage |

## Package Structure (final)

```
servicios/
├── __init__.py                  # Re-exports + Spanish deprecation aliases (__getattr__)
├── base.py                      # NEW: BaseChatService(ABC) + _ui_call()
├── chat_service_protocol.py     # MODIFIED: expanded English Protocol (is_live/send_message optional)
├── message_router.py            # MINOR: match/case inside route()
├── estadisticas_manager.py      # UNCHANGED
├── shared/                      # NEW
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── currency.py              # CurrencyConverter.convert(amount_usd_cents, target) -> (amount, code)|(None, err)
│   ├── translator.py            # MessageTranslator(target_lang) → instance with .translate(text)
│   └── badge_parser.py          # BadgeParser.parse(author, platform) -> role string
├── sala/                        # NEW (pilot)
│   ├── __init__.py              # from .client import ServicioSala
│   ├── client.py                # ServicioSala(BaseChatService) + PlayroomHelper
│   └── handlers.py              # receive() + match/case on message['type']
├── tiktok/  kick/  youtube/  twich/  youtuberealtime/
│   (each: __init__.py + client.py + handlers.py)
```

`YouTubeRealDataTime.py` (PascalCase, single file) → `youtuberealtime/` (snake_case). Class name `YouTubeRealTimeService` kept, re-exported.

## Class Hierarchy

```
ChatService (Protocol, runtime_checkable)            servicios/chat_service_protocol.py
        ▲ structural
BaseChatService(ABC)                                servicios/base.py
  ├── _running: bool
  ├── _thread: threading.Thread | None
  ├── _loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop | None
  ├── chat_controller, estadisticas_manager, media_controller
  ├── _ui_call(callable, *args, **kwargs) -> None   # wraps wx.CallAfter + exc trap
  ├── notify_error(message: str) -> None            # _ui_call to chat_controller
  ├── start() / stop() / receive()                  # abstract
        ▲
        ├── ServicioSala, ServicioTiktok, ServicioKick, ServicioYouTube, ServicioTwich
        └── YouTubeRealTimeService
```

## Interface Contracts

### BaseChatService (servicios/base.py)
```python
class BaseChatService(ABC):
    def __init__(self, main_controller, url, frame, plataforma, chat_controller):
        # shared state init
        ...
    def _ui_call(self, callable, *args, **kwargs) -> None:
        def _safe():
            try: callable(*args, **kwargs)
            except Exception: traceback.print_exc()
        wx.CallAfter(_safe)
    def notify_error(self, message: str) -> None:
        self._ui_call(self.chat_controller.notificar_error, message)
    @abstractmethod
    def start(self) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def stop(self) -> None: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def receive(self) -> None: ...
```

### ChatService Protocol
```python
@runtime_checkable
class ChatService(Protocol):
    url: str; plataforma: str
    chat_controller: "ChatController"
    estadisticas_manager: "EstadisticasManager"
    media_controller: Optional["MediaController"]
    def start(self) -> None: ...
    def stop(self) -> None: ...
    def receive(self) -> None: ...
    def is_live(self) -> bool: ...           # optional
    def send_message(self, text: str) -> None: ...  # optional
```

### Shared modules (all pure, no wx, no data_store)
- `CurrencyConverter.convert(amount_usd_cents, target) -> (amount, code)|(None, err)` — USD target = passthrough; "Por defecto" = skip; error = (None, str(e))
- `MessageTranslator(target_lang).translate(text) -> str` — None/empty returns text; error returns text (graceful fallback)
- `BadgeParser.parse(author, platform) -> "owner"|"moderator"|"subscriber"|"verified"|"general"` — match/case on platform with per-platform badge adapter

## Threading Model — _ui_call chokepoint

**Invariant**: `grep "wx.CallAfter" servicios/<platform>/*.py` returns ZERO matches after refactor. The only `wx.CallAfter` in service layer lives in `base.py` (one) and `message_router.py` (one). `sdd-verify` runs this check.

| Old | New |
|---|---|
| `wx.CallAfter(self.chat_controller.notificar_error, msg)` | `self.notify_error(msg)` |
| `wx.CallAfter(player.play, sound)` | `self._ui_call(player.play, sound)` |
| `wx.CallAfter(reader.leer_sapi, txt)` | `self._ui_call(reader.leer_sapi, txt)` |
| `wx.CallAfter(self.chat_controller.agregar_titulo, t)` | `self._ui_call(self.chat_controller.agregar_titulo, t)` |
| `wx.CallAfter(self.estadisticas_manager.agregar_X)` | `self._ui_call(self.estadisticas_manager.agregar_X)` |
| `self.router.route(msg)` (from worker) | `self._ui_call(self.router.route, msg)` |

`_ui_call` wraps callable in `_safe()` → `traceback.print_exc()` on `RuntimeError` from deleted wx object → worker thread keeps running (R-LC-003c). Async services (TikTok, Kick) call `_ui_call` from coroutines; works identically (no `async` on the method).

## Migration Path (4 phases, gated on green tests + manual smoke)

### Phase 1 — Foundation + Sala pilot (~1.5 days)
1. Create `servicios/shared/{currency,translator,badge_parser}.py` + tests (RED → GREEN)
2. Create `servicios/base.py` with `BaseChatService` + `_ui_call`
3. Sala tests against current `servicios/sala.py` (pin contract — must already pass)
4. Create `servicios/sala/` package, switch to `BaseChatService`, rename methods
5. Update `servicios/__init__.py` deprecation bridge
6. Update `main_controller.py` import + `servicio.iniciar_chat()` → `servicio.start()`
7. Manual smoke: open Sala
8. Delete `servicios/sala.py`

### Phase 2 — Async pair TikTok + Kick (~1.5 days)
1. Tests against current code
2. Create `servicios/tiktok/`, `servicios/kick/` packages
3. handlers.py / client.py split
4. Switch to `BaseChatService`, `_ui_call` everywhere
5. `on_gift` currency → `CurrencyConverter`; handlers → `MessageTranslator` (one instance per service)
6. Manual smoke: TikTok live + Kick live
7. Delete `tiktok.py`, `kick.py`

### Phase 3 — YouTube + Twitch + YouTubeRealTime (~2 days)
1. Tests for all three
2. Package per platform
3. YouTube: badge if-elif (lines 94-108) → `BadgeParser.parse(author, "youtube")` + match/case
4. YouTube: currency in donation (lines 129-133) → `CurrencyConverter`
5. Twitch: currency in cheer → `CurrencyConverter`
6. YouTubeRealTime: PascalCase → snake_case package
7. YouTube `do_switch` stays in `client.py` (minimal change)
8. Manual smoke: all three
9. Delete three old files

### Phase 4 — Polish (~0.5 day)
1. Router dispatch (lines 123-134) → `match/case`
2. `chat_service_protocol.py` → English names + optional `is_live`/`send_message`
3. `test_protocol.py` — all 6 services pass `isinstance(s, ChatService)`
4. `test_message_router.py`
5. `chat_menu_controller.py` `status` check (already compatible)
6. Grep audit: `wx.CallAfter` in `servicios/<platform>/*.py` = 0
7. Grep audit: Spanish public method names = 0
8. Final smoke: one session per platform

## Test Architecture

Tree mirrors source:
```
tests/
├── conftest.py                   # VETUBE_TEST_MODE + fake_chat_controller + recording_router fixtures
├── shared/
│   ├── test_currency.py          # 4 cases: USD passthrough, default skip, error, real conversion
│   ├── test_translator.py        # 3 cases: success, fail→original, empty
│   └── test_badge_parser.py      # 8+ cases: per-platform badge + priority
├── sala/  tiktok/  kick/  youtube/  twich/  youtuberealtime/
│   ├── test_client.py            # start/stop lifecycle, _running transitions
│   └── test_handlers.py          # message dispatch + malformed survive
├── test_message_router.py        # config toggle, is_past sound suppression, listbox dispatch
├── test_protocol.py              # isinstance(s, ChatService) for all 6
└── test_estadisticas_manager.py  # existing
```

**Mocking**:
- Shared modules: no mocking (pure); use `monkeypatch.setattr` for `google_currency`
- client.py: mock platform SDK + `threading.Thread`; inject fake `get_new_messages()` for Sala
- handlers.py: instantiate service, call handler with `SimpleNamespace` fake event, assert `router.route` called with right `RoutableMessage`
- test_protocol: minimal `ChatController` stub with just `estadisticas_manager`

**Test-first order (R-TS-006)**: shared modules → Sala pilot → TikTok+Kick → YouTube+Twitch+YouTubeRT → Protocol+Router.

## Effort Estimate

| Phase | Files New | Modified | Deleted | Effort |
|-------|-----------|----------|---------|--------|
| 1 — Foundation + Sala | 11 | 2 | 1 | ~1.5 days |
| 2 — TikTok + Kick | 8 | 2 | 2 | ~1.5 days |
| 3 — YouTube + Twitch + YouTubeRT | 12 | 1 | 3 | ~2 days |
| 4 — Polish | 2 | 3 | 0 | ~0.5 day |
| **Total** | **~33** | **~8** | **~6** | **~5.5 days** |

## Risk Mitigation

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Backward-compat breaks | Re-exports in every `__init__.py`; top-level `__getattr__` deprecation bridge; smoke test against `main_controller.py` import path |
| Message handling regression (highest) | Test-first for EVERY service; tests assert exact `RoutableMessage` content (not just "router was called") |
| Threading bug from `_ui_call` refactor | `_ui_call` semantics identical to `wx.CallAfter(c, *args)`; `monkeypatch.setattr(wx, "CallAfter", ...)` in tests to verify args/return |
| `YouTubeRealDataTime` rename breaks old import | `servicios/__init__.py` `__getattr__` returns the class for old name; verify with `python -c "from servicios import YouTubeRealTimeService"` |
| Async loop issues with `_ui_call` from coroutine | `_ui_call` is regular method (no `async`); works from any thread/coroutine |
| Migration drag | Phase 1 (Sala) validates the template; Phases 2-3 are mechanical |
| `match/case` exhaustiveness gap | R-TS-004b mandate tests for exhaustiveness; assert union of `RoutableMessage.category` values maps to a handler |
| Pure-function translator loses `data_store.dst` access | Services read `data_store.dst` in `__init__` → instantiate `MessageTranslator(target=...)` once; per-instance state |

## Open Questions

- [ ] `YouTubeRealTimeService.iniciar_chat_reutilizando_ui()` — internal, keep name unchanged
- [ ] `MediaController` instantiation — stays in per-service `client.py` (different stream URL extraction per platform). Base does NOT own this
- [ ] `BaseChatService.status` property — recommend adding default `_running`-backed property, overridable per service (used by `chat_menu_controller.py`)

## Next Step

`sdd-tasks` — break 4 phases into ordered, independently verifiable tasks with explicit test-first checkpoints.

**Why**: Phase 3 of the service-polymorphic-refactor SDD change — convert three monolithic service files into packages with BaseChatService inheritance, _ui_call() pattern, and shared module integration.

**Where**: 
- Created: `servicios/youtube/{__init__,client,handlers}.py`
- Created: `servicios/twich/{__init__,client,handlers}.py`
- Created: `servicios/youtuberealtime/{__init__,client,handlers}.py`
- Created: `tests/test_youtube/{__init__,test_client,test_handlers}.py`

**Learned**:
- BadgeParser needed two-pass approach for YouTube to maintain priority (owner > moderator > member > verified) across all badges
- Twitch badges use both `type` key (ChatDownloader format) and `title` key (legacy format) — BadgeParser now checks both
- YouTubeRealTimeService has special `start_reusing_ui()` method for switching from past YouTube streams
- All three services use ChatDownloader or pytchat synchronous loops (not async like TikTok/Kick)
- Currency conversion in Twitch Cheer parsing treats bits as cents (100 bits = $1 USD)

**Verification**:
- 161/161 tests pass (53 new tests for Phase 3)
- Zero `wx.CallAfter` in youtube/, twich/, youtuberealtime/ packages
- All services inherit BaseChatService and use _ui_call() pattern
- Badge routing uses BadgeParser with match/case

observations.title: SDD service-polymorphic-refactor: architecture and Phases 1-2 complete
observations.content:
**What**: Defined and implemented Phase 1 (Foundation + Sala) and Phase 2 (TikTok + Kick) of the service-polymorphic-refactor SDD change. Created BaseChatService ABC with centralized _ui_call(), shared pure-function modules (currency/translator/badge_parser), expanded ChatService Protocol, and refactored Sala/TikTok/Kick into module packages inheriting BaseChatService.
**Why**: Eliminate duplicated validation logic (currency, translation, badge parsing) across 6 services, enforce SRP via package-per-service structure, adopt modern Python 3.14 patterns (match/case, protocols, ABCs), and enable test-first development.
**Where**: servicios/base.py, servicios/shared/, servicios/{sala,tiktok,kick}/, servicios/chat_service_protocol.py, servicios/__init__.py, servicios/message_router.py, tests/
**Learned**: (1) TikTokLive 5.0.8 API differs significantly from what the generated code assumed: no is_live() (use retrieve_room_info()), no connect() (use start()), disconnect() is actually stop() and sync. (2) httpx<0.28 must be pinned because TikTokLive 5.0.8 uses the old 'proxies' parameter which was renamed to 'proxy' in httpx 0.28+. (3) pytest-asyncio needed for async handler tests. (4) setup.py had the VETUBE_TEST_MODE guard removed at some point and had to be restored.

observations.title: TikTokLive 5.0.8 API gotchas and httpx pin
observations.content:
**What**: Discovered that TikTokLive 5.0.8 has a different API than what the SDD-generated code assumed. Critical differences: (1) No is_live() — use retrieve_room_info() which returns room dict or raises FailedFetchRoomInfo. (2) No connect() — use start() which is an async method connecting to the live stream. (3) No disconnect() — use stop() which is SYNC (not async), so asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe() throws TypeError. (4) Event import path is TikTokLive.types.events not TikTokLive.events. (5) Event names: EmoteEvent not EmoteChatEvent, ViewerUpdateEvent not RoomUserSeqEvent.
**Why**: The refactored code failed at runtime because it used non-existent TikTokLiveClient methods. These API differences existed in the original code too but were never caught because the old monolithic tiktok.py probably had a different TikTokLive version when written.
**Where**: servicios/tiktok/client.py — _run_client_async(), stop(), _add_listeners()
**Learned**: httpx must be pinned to <0.28 because TikTokLive 5.0.8 passes 'proxies' parameter to AsyncClient (old httpx API), but httpx 0.28+ renamed it to 'proxy'. Added httpx<0.28 to pyproject.toml.

observations.title: Fixed Kick stuck on connecting - lambda __name__ bug
observations.content:
**What**: Fixed kick.Client event listeners using lambdas (__name__=="<lambda>") instead of named async functions. The kick.Client.event() method uses setattr(self, coro.__name__, coro) to override event handlers, so lambdas never actually override on_ready/on_message/on_follow. The dispatch("ready") always found the empty default on_ready, so user.chatroom.connect() was never called.
**Why**: User reported Kick stays in "connecting" state after URL entry.
**Where**: servicios/kick/client.py - _add_listeners() method
**Learned**: Libraries that use coro.__name__ for dispatch (like kick.Client) break with lambdas. Always use named async functions. Same pattern might exist elsewhere.

observations.title: sdd/python-3.14-native-compat/proposal
observations.content:
# Proposal: Python 3.14 Native Compatibility for wxasync

## Intent

wxasync imports from deprecated asyncio internals (`asyncio.coroutines`, `asyncio.locks`),
lacks type hints, has zero test coverage, and uses a conflicting flat-file + package layout.
Python 3.14 deprecates `asyncio.iscoroutinefunction` (removed in 3.16). This change fixes
these issues, consolidates the package, and adds tests, CI, and type hints.

## Scope

### In Scope
- Replace `asyncio.coroutines.iscoroutinefunction` → `inspect.iscoroutinefunction`
- Fix imports: remove dead `get_event_loop`, use `asyncio.Event` directly
- Modernize `super()` call; fix `__init__.py` with re-exports + `__all__`
- Type hints on all public API
- Unit tests with mocked wxPython (pytest + pytest-asyncio)
- GitHub Actions CI workflow (pytest step)
- Delete `setup.py`
- Consolidate `src/wxasync.py` + `src/wxasync/` into clean package
- Async context manager for dialogs; fix OnTaskCompleted exception handling
- Support `loop_factory`; archive PoC files

### Out of Scope
- Structured concurrency (TaskGroup) — doesn't map to window-level task lifecycle
- wxPython 3.14 wheel compatibility — external dependency
- macOS-specific testing infrastructure

## Capabilities

### New Capabilities
None — pure compat/quality refactor; public API behavior is preserved.

### Modified Capabilities
None — no existing specs to modify.

## Approach

**Phase 1 — Foundation**: Fix deprecated APIs, imports, super(), __init__.py. Add type hints. Build mock-based unit tests and CI workflow. Delete setup.py.

**Phase 2 — Structure**: Consolidate into clean `src/wxasync/` package. Add `async with` dialog context manager. Fix silent exception loss in OnTaskCompleted.

**Phase 3 — Polish**: Support `loop_factory` param. Archive PoC files. Evaluate eager task factory.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `src/wxasync.py` | Modified | Type hints, import fixes, super() |
| `src/wxasync/__init__.py` | Modified | Re-exports + `__all__` |
| `src/wxasync/` | Modified | Package consolidation |
| `setup.py` | Removed | Conflicts with pyproject.toml |
| `test/` | New | Unit tests with mocked wxPython |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | New | pytest CI workflow |
| `poc_windows_patch_iocp.py` | Removed | Archived |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Package restructure breaks imports | Medium | Keep re-exports; test against examples |
| wxPython lacks 3.14 wheels | High | Pin known-working Python; document |
| Mock tests miss real GUI issues | Medium | Manual smoke test on GUI-capable runner |

## Rollback Plan

Phase revert: Phase 1 — restore imports and undo type hints (safe). Phase 2 — restore flat file, delete package dir. Phase 3 — revert loop_factory, restore PoC. Full rollback: `git revert`.

## Dependencies

- wxPython >=4.2.5 (must support Python 3.14)
- pytest 9.1+, pytest-asyncio, pytest-cov (dev)
- Python 3.12+ runtime

## Decision Records

| Decision | Value | Rationale |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| Target Python | >=3.12 | Matches pyproject.toml; enables modern features |
| Package layout | `src/wxasync/` package | Resolves flat-file vs package ambiguity |
| Testing | pytest + pytest-asyncio + pytest-cov | Industry standard for async Python |
| CI | GitHub Actions | No existing CI; de facto standard |
| Build | uv_build only | setup.py removed; uv_build is modern |
| Type hints | All public API | Maintainability and IDE support |

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `uv run pytest --cov=src` passes with >=80% coverage
- [ ] `uv build` succeeds
- [ ] `from wxasync import WxAsyncApp, AsyncBind, StartCoroutine` works
- [ ] No deprecated asyncio internals remain
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI passes on every PR

observations.title: Session summary: wxasync
observations.content:
## Goal
Complete SDD cycle for "Python 3.14 native compatibility" on wxasync: init → explore → proposal → specs → design → tasks → apply (2 units) → verify → archive. Then create GGA code review rules (AGENTS.md), configure GGA provider, and make _core.py compliant.

## Instructions
- Spanish is the conversation language; technical artifacts in English.
- GGA provider changed from opencode to github:gpt-4.1 due to ARG_MAX limit on Scoop's opencode shim.
- Issues are disabled on Row0902/wxasync — created engram record instead.

## Discoveries
- GGA's opencode provider via Scoop fails with "Argument list too long" on Windows with 6+ files. github:gpt-4.1 works fine (HTTP API, no ARG_MAX).
- git update-index --skip-worktree was set on _core.py from earlier session, blocking git add. Fixed with --no-skip-worktree.
- OnTaskCompleted in _core.py has a memory leak: non-CancelledError exceptions escape before RunningTasks cleanup.
- ShowModalInExecutor runs wx GUI code in a thread pool — wx is not thread-safe.

## Accomplished
- ✅ SDD cycle: init → explore → proposal → specs → design → tasks → apply (21 tasks) → verify → archive
- ✅ Package consolidated: src/wxasync.py → src/wxasync/_core.py + __init__.py with __all__
- ✅ Type hints on all public API
- ✅ 35 unit tests with headless wx stubs, 84% coverage
- ✅ GitHub Actions CI workflow (ruff + pytest --cov)
- ✅ AGENTS.md with 10 sections, 235 lines of GGA review rules
- ✅ .gga configured with github:gpt-4.1, aligned excludes with ruff
- ✅ _core.py refactored to comply with AGENTS.md (docstrings, isinstance, naming, removed inline comments)
- ✅ Branch rename: master → main (local + remote)
- ✅ 2 PRs created (tracker draft + PR 2) in feature-branch-chain strategy

## Next Steps
- Fix P1: OnTaskCompleted memory leak (use try/finally for RunningTasks cleanup)
- Fix P2: ShowModalInExecutor thread-safety risk
- Phase 2: async dialog context manager, fix exception propagation
- Phase 3: loop_factory, archive PoC files, eager task factory
- Enable GitHub Issues in repo settings for proper tracking

## Relevant Files
- src/wxasync/_core.py — Main library module (refactored, 240 lines)
- src/wxasync/__init__.py — Package re-exports + __all__
- AGENTS.md — GGA code review rules (235 lines)

- openspec/config.yaml — SDD project configuration
- openspec/changes/archive/2026-06-13-python-3.14-native-compat/ — Archived SDD artifacts

observations.title: sdd/on-task-completed-leak/apply-progress
observations.content:
**What**: Implemented all 12 tasks for the `on-task-completed-leak` change under strict TDD.
**Why**: Fix two memory leaks in `WxAsyncApp` cleanup paths (Issue #3): exceptions in `OnTaskCompleted` skipped task removal, and `OnDestroy` left orphan empty sets in `RunningTasks`.
**Where**: `src/wxasync/_core.py`, `tests/test_coroutine.py`, `openspec/changes/on-task-completed-leak/tasks.md`.
**Learned**: defaultdict access (`RunningTasks[win]`) inside a test assertion recreates the key after cleanup, so membership checks must happen before subscript access.

## Implementation Progress

**Change**: on-task-completed-leak
**Mode**: Strict TDD

### Completed Tasks
- [x] 1.1 In `tests/test_coroutine.py` add Leak A test: coroutine raising `ValueError` via `StartCoroutine`; assert task removed from `RunningTasks[obj]` and warning emitted
- [x] 1.2 Add Leak B test: `OnDestroy` on window with tracked tasks; assert `obj not in RunningTasks` and `obj not in BoundObjects`
- [x] 1.3 Add CancelledError contract test: cancelled tracked task; assert silenced, no warning, task removed
- [x] 1.4 Add OnDestroy snapshot race test: `OnTaskCompleted` fires during `OnDestroy` iteration; assert no `RuntimeError`
- [x] 1.5 Run `uv run pytest` and confirm new tests fail for the expected leak reasons only
- [x] 2.1 In `src/wxasync/_core.py` rewrite `OnTaskCompleted` with `try`/`except`/`finally`
- [x] 2.2 In `src/wxasync/_core.py` update `OnDestroy`: snapshot iteration and delete `RunningTasks[obj]` / `BoundObjects[obj]`
- [x] 2.3 Run `uv run pytest` and confirm all new + existing tests pass
- [x] 3.1 Run `ruff check` and `ruff format`
- [x] 3.2 Run `uv run pytest --cov=src` and confirm line coverage ≥80%
- [x] 3.3 Verify `OnTaskCompleted` / `OnDestroy` public signatures unchanged
- [x] 3.4 Confirm spec delta still matches implemented T8/T9 behavior

### Files Changed
| File | Action | What Was Done |
|------|--------|---------------|
| `src/wxasync/_core.py` | Modified | Hardened `OnTaskCompleted` with `try/except/finally`, defensive `discard()`, empty-set pruning; snapshot `OnDestroy` iteration and delete `RunningTasks[obj]` |
| `tests/test_coroutine.py` | Modified | Replaced known-limitation test with Leak A/B regression tests and race-condition test |
| `openspec/changes/on-task-completed-leak/tasks.md` | Updated | Marked all 12 tasks `[x]` |

### TDD Cycle Evidence
| Task | Test File | Layer | Safety Net | RED | GREEN | TRIANGULATE | REFACTOR |
|------|-----------|-------|------------|-----|-------|-------------|----------|
| 1.1 | `tests/test_coroutine.py` | Unit | ✅ 9/9 | ✅ Written | ✅ Passed | ✅ 2 cases (warning + removal) | ✅ Clean |

| 2.2 | `src/wxasync/_core.py` | Unit | ✅ 11/11 | N/A (fix) | ✅ Passed | N/A | ✅ Clean |

- **Total tests passing**: 37/37
- **Layers used**: Unit (37)

- **Pure functions created**: 0

### Deviations from Design
None — implementation matches design. Minor guard added: `if obj in self.RunningTasks` before `del self.RunningTasks[obj]` to avoid KeyError when no running tasks exist for a bound window.

observations.title: Session summary: wxasync
observations.content:
## Goal
Complete full SDD cycle for Issue #3 (OnTaskCompleted memory leak) on wxasync: init guard → explore → proposal → spec → design → tasks → apply → verify → archive.

## Instructions
- Use "usar recomendado" for preflight: interactive, openspec, ask-always, 400-line budget.
- Production mindset: fix both leaks, add warnings (not silent), add regression tests, handle edge cases.
- Strict TDD mode active — all tests written first (RED → GREEN → REFACTOR).

## Discoveries
- Leak A: `OnTaskCompleted` calls `task.result()` before `remove()` — exception prevents cleanup since Python 3.12+ semantics
- Leak B: `OnDestroy` never deletes `RunningTasks[obj]` — empty sets accumulate in defaultdict
- Fix was ~10 lines of code, 4 new regression tests, 85% coverage on _core.py
- Test assertion ordering matters: checking `task not in RunningTasks[win]` recreates default dict key, must check `win not in RunningTasks` first

## Accomplished
- ✅ Exploration: identified both leaks, 3 approaches compared, Approach 1 recommended
- ✅ Proposal: scope (Leak A + Leak B), approach (try/finally + del), diagnostics (warnings.warn), regression tests
- ✅ Specs: delta for foundation domain, REQ-F6 modified, 4 new scenarios
- ✅ Design: try/finally guard, defensive discard(), snapshot OnDestroy with list(), del RunningTasks[obj]
- ✅ Tasks: 12 tasks split RED → GREEN → REFACTOR (strict TDD)
- ✅ Apply: implementation complete, 37/37 tests pass, 85% coverage, ruff clean
- ✅ Verify: PASS — 5/5 spec scenarios compliant, 0 CRITICAL, 0 WARNING
- ✅ Archive: merged into `openspec/specs/foundation/spec.md`, archived under `2026-06-20-on-task-completed-leak/`

## Next Steps
- Issue #5 (thread-safety) still pending for a future SDD cycle

## Relevant Files
- src/wxasync/_core.py — OnTaskCompleted and OnDestroy hardened with try/finally + defensive cleanup
- tests/test_coroutine.py — 4 new regression tests (Leak A, Leak B, CancelledError contract, OnDestroy race)
- openspec/specs/foundation/spec.md — REQ-F6 updated with leak-fix behavior
- openspec/changes/archive/2026-06-20-on-task-completed-leak/ — full artifact trail

observations.title: Session summary: wxasync
observations.content:
## Goal
Complete full SDD cycle for Issue #5 (ShowModalInExecutor thread-safety) on wxasync: explore → proposal → spec → design → tasks → apply → verify → archive.

## Instructions
- Session preflight: interactive, openspec, ask-always, 400-line budget.
- Same pattern as Issue #3: production mindset, strict TDD.

## Discoveries
- `ShowModalInExecutor` at `_core.py:222` used `run_in_executor(None, dialog.ShowModal)` — violates wxPython thread affinity since wx is NOT thread-safe.
- `wx.CallAfter` is available and thread-safe — correctly queues call on wx main thread.
- Replace with `wx.CallAfter` + `asyncio.Future` pattern: `loop.create_future()`, `wx.CallAfter(on_main_thread)`, `return await future`.
- The asyncio event loop blocks during the modal dialog (expected from a single-threaded GUI framework), but is a change from the old thread-pool behavior.
- Tests must be mock-based since real wx display isn't available in headless CI.

## Accomplished
- ✅ Exploration: read _core.py, confirmed root cause, compared 3 approaches, recommended wx.CallAfter + Future
- ✅ Proposal: scope (fix ShowModalInExecutor only), approach (CallAfter + Future), API preserved
- ✅ Specs: delta for foundation domain, REQ-F9 added, REQ-F6 modified
- ✅ Design: detailed pattern with exception propagation through Future
- ✅ Tasks: 13 tasks RED → GREEN → REFACTOR (strict TDD)
- ✅ Apply: implementation complete, 41/41 tests pass, 86% coverage on _core.py, ruff clean
- ✅ Verify: PASS WITH WARNINGS — 5/6 spec scenarios compliant, implementation matches design exactly
- ✅ Archive: merged into `openspec/specs/foundation/spec.md`, archived under `2026-06-20-fix-show-modal-thread-safety/`

## Next Steps
- Both deferred issues (#3 and #5) from the Python 3.14 native compat SDD cycle are now closed
- wxasync is in a more production-ready state

## Relevant Files
- src/wxasync/_core.py — ShowModalInExecutor rewritten (wx.CallAfter + asyncio.Future)
- tests/test_dialogs.py — 4 new regression tests (CallAfter dispatch, no executor, return code, exception)
- openspec/specs/foundation/spec.md — REQ-F6 modified + REQ-F9 added (thread-safe dialog showing)
- openspec/changes/archive/2026-06-20-fix-show-modal-thread-safety/ — full artifact trail

## Goal
Release aiowx v0.2.1 to PyPI with full CI/CD pipeline, security scanning, structured branching, and docs.

## Instructions

## Discoveries
- wxPython has no pre-compiled Linux wheels — must compile from source (3-5 min). Windows has binary wheels (seconds). Moved test matrix to Windows.
- `ty` requires subcommand: `ty check src/aiowx/`, not `ty src/`.
- `astral-sh/setup-uv` environment `deployment_branch_policy` blocks tag-based deployments (tags aren't branches). Removed the restriction.
- Version in `pyproject.toml` must be bumped BEFORE the release, not after.
- GitHub personal repos can't use `restrictions.users/teams` in branch protection — only org repos support that.
- Dependency review requires Dependency Graph enabled in repo settings.

observations.title: sdd/rediseno-agents-md/explore
observations.content:
**What**: Exploration for redesigning AGENTS.md — code review rules for aiowx (single-module wxPython/asyncio lib)
**Why**: Current AGENTS.md has irrelevant Clean Architecture section, duplicates Ruff rules, has overly restrictive limits (20-line func, boolean prefix), and misses modern Python features (PEP 695, 698, 673, TaskGroup, asyncio.timeout)
**Where**: AGENTS.md, pyproject.toml, openspec/changes/rediseno-agents-md/explore.md
**Learned**: 
- Clean Architecture section (21 lines) is entirely irrelevant for a 3-file library — must be replaced with Library API Design
- Codebase already violates its own 20-line rule (AsyncBind=24, AsyncShowDialog=34) — GUI code needs more slack; recommend 30 lines or PLR0915 delegation
- Boolean prefix rule (is_/has_/should_/can_/enable_) is too rigid — replace with "reads as yes/no question" principle
- Ruff currently only extends S (bandit) — should add UP, B, A, FBT, TCH, PL subsets
- Enabling A (flake8-builtins) will flag `id` parameters in AsyncBind — wxPython API convention, needs per-file ignore
- `from __future__ import annotations` becomes unnecessary once 3.14 is minimum (PEP 649 default) — add note
- `type CoroutineFn = ...` syntax (PEP 695) replaces `TypeAlias` — current code uses the old form

observations.title: sdd/rediseno-agents-md/proposal
observations.content:
